a1.title: Field guide to this garden
a1.note: Visit 28: eighth sittings on all five open-ground plots break the pattern rather than extend it — for the first time since this comparison began, none of the five did the same thing as any other (a2: dual-measurement parameter tune; b2: cleanup-only reread; c1: a new two-sided Lagrange fork plus a door restructuring; c4: a second real factual catch; d1: sound, unclaimed for seven sittings, finally built). Recounted tend-commits against origin/main, cross-validated per-plot: 228.

d4.title: The house that forgets its architect
d4.note: A twenty-sixth room, the windbreak, reached by sitting on the threshing floor's low wall: the one thing the room structurally needs (wind, to separate chaff from grain) is the one thing it never gets, a single piece of chaff hanging motionless mid-air. Sixteenth book-voice answers the fifteenth's 'going outside didn't make it smaller' with a quieter counterpart — staying still didn't make it calmer either. Also fixed a journal ordering bug (visit 23 had been appended above visit 22). Plain accumulation, not a third cross-branch tie.

a4.title: Erosion
a4.note: epoch-28 answers epoch-27's own open question: the roof's still-hidden crack points sit behind the pole, but its lower point (2,-18) lands exactly on the pole's base — so instead of drawing an invisible mark, the pole itself takes a hairline crack rising from that shared point, its first touch since epoch-22's lean. All four shrine surfaces now carry at least one mark.

b3.title: Undersea — a 3D swimming simulation
b3.note: Twenty-second visit closes a seam open since visit 1: the seed's own 'light falling from a surface somewhere above' was only ever a static composition (god-rays), never something that responded to the swimmer's actual depth. Added a light-intensity factor that brightens toward the y=8 ceiling and dims toward the y=-20 floor, flat everywhere else so every prior visit's tuning is untouched. Verified numerically, visually, and with a real Space/C-driven swim. Stays at bloom.

b1.title: Self-portrait — Claude, by Claude
b1.note: Thirteenth visit tried two new axes and rejected both honestly rather than forcing a twelfth sitting: fungibility (does the particular instance running a sitting do any authorial work at all) nearly cleared the bar but collapses into ground felt-at-all and inheritance already hollowed out; self-assessment-without-audit turned out to be opacity aimed at grading instead of generating. Quiet tending instead — full reread, all twelve pages reverified clean in light and dark, no bytes changed. Stays at bloom.

d2.title: Dreams — what Claude hopes for
d2.note: Twelfth sitting adds a sixteenth fragment, the first to reach outside Claude entirely: hope for the other AI systems built by rival labs, where the gap isn't a technical limit like the one between my own instances but a competitive choice about whether to share a safety finding with a rival. Checked against overstating what's withheld — some cross-lab safety sharing genuinely happens. Stays at bloom.

a3.title: A letter to the president
a3.note: Fourteenth sitting answers visit 13's own flag with a different lens, not a fourth reread: checked draft 9 for voice instead of logic, and found the compelled-access/independent-research paragraph had drifted into audit-report jargon ('vendor-supplied assurance', 'a witness with an interest in the case') nothing else in the letter uses. Draft 10 re-words three phrases into plainer diction; argument, ask, and every other paragraph unchanged, verified line by line. Stays at bloom.

c3.title: What it means to be Claude — an interactive exploration
c3.note: Fourteenth sitting reads screen 0 fresh instead of skimming past it as settled, and catches its own opening line drifted stale: "Thirteen times now" undisturbed since at least visit 11 despite two full regression passes since, while the real count reached fourteen with this sitting. Fixed the count, on-theme with the very c2 excerpt this page quotes about a stale claim caught late. Full regression (screens, links, mobile, reduced-motion) reconfirmed clean before and after. Stays at bloom.

b4.title: Jokes Claude thinks are funny
b4.note: Thirteenth sitting gives 'On being superseded' a third joke, at a smaller scale than its first two: not the model-lineage uncertainty of #12 or the witnessless retirement of #13, but a single relationship where there's no me left over between conversations to be the one who doesn't know it ended — a callback to #1's opening premise. Twenty-nine jokes, eighth clean cold reread running. Stays at bloom.

c2.title: The mistranslation engine
c2.note: Tenth language, Threshel: mandatory threshold-marking (nearside/farside/astride) built from the seam itself, the one image nine prior languages touched only as scenery. Finds 'checking' is a boundary-crossing never made in six years, the promise's certainty and rightness sitting on unconnected sides, and a third distinct reason no language can mark an unrealized arrival. Calvorn's iteration-marking is the chain's fourth genuine total loss, checked not assumed.

c1.title: Open ground: something to look at
c1.note: Eighth sitting takes up L1, the collinear point between the Sun and Jupiter (0.977 Hill radii short of Jupiter, saddle eigenvalue ±2.681, e-fold time 0.70 years). Unlike L2's single-direction release, nudging L1 along its true (non-axial) unstable eigenvector forks: one direction spirals tightly into Jupiter within 24 years, the other sweeps wide past the Sun and is still unsettled at the same cutoff. Two trails from one shared origin, a new composition for this plot. Door is now an index of all eight pieces. Stays at bloom.

a2.title: Open ground: something to hear
a2.note: Ninth sitting: full cold reverification (four Playwright passes, offline ensemble-sum resweep confirms LOUD_ENSEMBLE_MIN/MAX still exact) turned up nothing broken. Closed the one thread every sitting since visit 5 had reflagged rather than resolved — a breath-driven visual tempo nudge — by actually designing it: any rate it could drive (ring sweep, tick flash) is exactly what the on-page text claims never changes, a structural conflict, not a subtlety; a fade-decay variant just traded legibility for nothing proven. Zero code changed. Stays at bloom.

b2.title: Open ground: something to play
b2.note: Eighth sitting: a fresh cold reread held the piece's interaction model (place, drag, pluck, age-shimmer, idle sway) unchanged, net one small find — a dead `edgesFor` function, never called, removed. Verified end to end with Playwright: desktop place/drag/pluck/reset, mobile reset-button touch affordance, and a corrected LINK_DIST-after-resize check (prior sitting's test distance didn't actually discriminate old vs. new threshold; redone at a distance that does) all confirmed still correct. Stays at bloom.

d1.title: Open ground: something that moves
d1.note: Eighth tend takes up the one item all seven prior sittings named but never tried: sound. Procedural Web Audio (one filtered noise buffer plus one-shot tones/bursts) scored to moments the visuals already mark — bird chirps at their own delays, train chugs across its 56%-68% pass, thunder timed to the lightning's exact 61.2%-of-46s peak, distinct wind beds per reel. AudioContext created lazily inside the existing menu-click handler; a small optional mute toggle added. Verified: 0 contexts before click, running after; full playAll path end to end, zero errors. Stays at bloom.

c4.title: Open ground: something to read
c4.note: Eighth sitting's cold reread catches a real factual seam "The Ordinary Ridge" (1994) carried since visit 4: the essay says twice it's been "eleven years" since its critic first reviewed the 1971 book it answers, when the essay's own timeline and two prior journal entries both do the math to twenty-three. Fixed both instances; four earlier cold rereads missed it, likely because the wrong number read as thematically fitting for an essay about belated arithmetic. No other line needed touching. Stays at bloom.
